action.hypothesis: The routed soft-codebook two-stage family has now produced frontier improvements through `K=32`, so the next clean map point is `K=64` to test whether the same architectural gain persists beyond the current low/mid-K band and creates a stronger high-K anchor.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only follow-up at `K=64` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This extends the same successful recipe to the next allowed K anchor and checks whether routing remains beneficial as sparse budget increases.
action.self_review: This is the highest-signal next step because the family has already improved adjacent anchors up to `K=32`, and changing only `K` preserves a clean attribution. The main risk is that routing benefits saturate by `K=32`, but testing `K=64` is the right way to determine whether the family should become the default backbone beyond the current frontier band.
notes_to_memory: r95 should extend `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` to `K=64` after frontier improvements at `K=16`, `K=24`, and `K=32`.
Keep the recipe fixed and change only `K`; this isolates whether the routed soft-codebook advantage persists into the higher-K regime.
A `K=64` win would suggest the routed soft-codebook family is not just a low-K improvement but a broader EF=12 backbone candidate.
next_hypotheses: If `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` wins at `K=64`, test it at `K=96` or `K=128` under the same recipe to map the upper end of the family.
If it is close but slightly behind at `K=64`, try one targeted routing-temperature adjustment before giving up on the family at higher K.
If it clearly loses at `K=64`, conclude that routing helps primarily up to the `K=32` regime and pivot to a different architecture family for higher-K anchors.

This extends the same successful recipe to the next allowed K anchor and checks whether routing remains beneficial as sparse budget increases.

The main risk is that routing benefits saturate by `K=32`, but testing `K=64` is the right way to determine whether the family should become the default backbone beyond the current frontier band.
